reasoning: While the fidelity composite score of 0.89 indicates strong overall quality, the specific defects on page 23 and page 60 are functional rather than cosmetic. Specifically, the incorrect transcription of 'pass/fail' accessibility indicators in a technical table (page 23) and the significant omission of contact information and links (page 60) provide misleading or incomplete data to the end-user. Given the cost asymmetry where bad documents are 3x more costly to auto-approve, these content-specific errors require human intervention.

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: In the 'Get in touch' section on page 60, significant contact information is missing. The source lists four primary contact details: Company name, Website, Phone, and Email. The output omits the website ('Web: www.ablr360.com') and the email ('Email: kim.casey@ablr360.com'). Additionally, the instruction text 'Scan QR code to Ablr contact page' is missing. This constitutes an omission of approximately 40% of the textual information within this specific contact section, exceeding the 25% threshol
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
Web: www.ablr360.com
Email: kim.casey@ablr360.com
Scan QR code to Ablr contact page
concerns.output_quote: The text is entirely absent from the corresponding section in the output.

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: In the 'Accessible color combinations' table on page 23, there are transcription errors in the pass/fail indicators ('Aa'). Specifically, in the row for 'Black background #000000', the output incorrectly leaves the cell for 'Light blue text #348AD7' empty (fail) when the source shows it as 'Aa' (pass). Conversely, it incorrectly places 'Aa' in the cell for 'Navy text #092940' when the source shows it as empty. This provides incorrect accessibility guidance compared to the source material.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Table on page 23, row 'Black background #000000': Light blue text column contains 'Aa'; Navy text column is empty.
concerns.output_quote: Table at reading_order 150, row 1: Cell 4 (Light blue) is empty; Cell 6 (Navy) contains 'Aa'.

concerns.analysis: On page 60, the reading order is logically flawed and incomplete. The heading 'Thank You!', which acts as a concluding remark in the source, is placed between the 'Get in touch' heading and the actual contact details. This disrupts the intended flow where the 'Get in touch' section should immediately present the contact information. Furthermore, three distinct blocks of text ('Web: www.ablr360.com', 'Email: kim.casey@ablr360.com', and 'Scan QR code to Ablr contact page') are omitted from the str
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 60 layout shows 'Get in touch' followed by contact info, with 'Thank You!' as a separate concluding element on the right.

concerns.analysis: On page 60, critical contact links are missing. While the phone number is preserved, the website (www.ablr360.com) and email address (kim.casey@ablr360.com) are absent from the output, failing to provide the actionable items present in the source.
concerns.output_quote: The text blocks and associated links for the website and email are missing from the elements list for Page 60.

action_items: Correct the 'Aa' pass/fail indicators in the color contrast table on page 23 to match the source document
Restore missing website, email, and QR code text in the 'Get in touch' section on page 60
Fix the reading order on page 60 so that contact details follow the 'Get in touch' heading properly
